75478.subject:
Action required: your billing account 01FB74-1CEEDC-1297AD is past
 due or has invalid payment information.
75478.body:
Google Cloud Platform Go to my console »





Action required


Dear Google customer,


You are receiving this email because you are a Google Cloud Platform,  
Firebase, or API customer.


Our records indicate that your billing account: 01FB74-1CEEDC-1297AD is  
past due or does not have valid payment information associated with it.  
This may happen if your credit card has expired or was cancelled, and we  
haven't received valid payment information from you.

Please update your billing account with valid payment information by  
following this link:

https://console.cloud.google.com/billing/01FB74-1CEEDC-1297AD/settings


Failure to make payments may result in suspension and/or termination of  
your billing account and the related Project(s) or Service(s). Note that  
some services might already be impacted. If you have already updated your  
payment information, please disregard this message.

If you have any questions please contact us using this form:  
https://support.google.com/code/contact/cloud_platform_billing







The Google team





Google Plus Twitter Blogger



© 2026 Google LLC, 1600 Amphitheatre Parkway, Mountain View, CA 94043

You have received this mandatory service announcement to update you about  
important changes to Google Cloud Platform, Firebase, APIs, or to your  
account.

75472.subject: How much will a 4th of July cookout cost this year?
75472.body:
<link rel=stylesheet href=https://marber-cdn.economist.com/foundations/latest/css/font-face.css>

Anna Kerr

Senior audience editor



 Our cover this week wishes America a happy 250th birthday. We have a host of articles in our 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5Mjd9AAcAAAAAA5NZR9aubk9K31JoWqUpRfb00k3u_nwH2qKRoJDdG-BOk3eB_qQoYS-56h42GpXshbidzGyXv6Hn1Jpqd9BZdp7W82CXJd_NKliuF58 
weekly issue  
 reflecting on the milestone. In one we 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5Mjd9AAcAAAAAA5NZR9avDYFNEFzNbquVSalNflevjc-WIstvumhoyQJyZ5tWCJUG1QFJm_jU7g2OuoZlwHNaOtmZBXGnJA7pnOcrAs0X2twcgC00MP0 
calculate how much  
 a 4th of July cookout will cost this year—and explain what might be pushing up the BBQ bill. 


 Another big anniversary this week: on July 1st the Chinese Communist Party marked the 105th anniversary of its founding. But

75471.subject: 📰 AI-curated Daily Crypto Digest — July 4
75471.body:
AI-Curated Daily Crypto Digest

Автор: Oleh Bezuhlyi


Автор: Oleh Bezuhlyi

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/ai-curated-daily-crypto-digest-july-4-oleh-bezuhlyi-yryhf?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3Bb7V4oAp8Ste1Sn72h54D%2Fg%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=37781b7Fefvck1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mr6kd5ei~ox-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mr6kd5ei-ox
  
🧠 AI's Crypto News Brief: Bitcoin clawed back above $62K after touching multi-year lows, but the mood stays cautious — ETF outflows, a USDT delisting, and tightening rules across the UK and EU keep regulation firmly in the driver's seat. 🇬🇧 UK Unveils Sweeping Crypto Rules to Court Global Trading Britain's FCA rolled out a comprehensive digital-asset framework designed to open the country to global crypto liquidity and institutional players. Analysts praised the ambition but warned the authorization process could prove daunting, leaving major compliance hurdles that may slow the rollout. Read full story 💳 Revolut to Delist Tether's USDT by August 31 Europe's largest fintech notified customers it will drop USDT after August 31, citing regulatory and risk concerns. Users can buy for a few more days and withdraw until the deadline, after which any remaining balances are automatically converted into their base fiat currency. More details 🇪🇺 EU Moves to Bar Retail Investors From Prediction Markets European regulators signalled a crackdown on the multibillion-dollar prediction-market boom, stressing that a product's function as a derivative — not its branding — determines compliance. The move would wall retail traders off from one of crypto's hottest 2026 growth areas. Read more 🏦 Germany's Sparkassen to Offer Crypto Inside Banking Apps Germany's vast Sparkassen savings-bank network and cooperative banks are preparing to let everyday customers trade crypto directly through their standard banking apps. Framed under MiCA, the rollout could accelerate mainstream adoption across Europe and pressure rival banks to follow. See details 📈 BlackRock Puts Tokenization at Center of $400B Private-Markets Push BlackRock is expanding aggressively into private markets with a $400 billion fundraising goal, placing tokenization at the heart of the strategy. The move underscores how the world's largest asset manager sees blockchain steadily reshaping traditional finance. Read full story ⚖️ US Sheriffs Drop Opposition to CLARITY Act, Boosting Senate Odds The Major County Sheriffs of America shifted from opposition to neutral on the digital-asset CLARITY Act, removing a key roadblock as the bill advances through the Senate. The group still wants amendments giving local law enforcement more resources to chase illicit-finance cases. More details 📉 Bitc
